channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I talked to two people today about rent. — 50 replies — 176.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I talked to two people today about rent.

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-16 01:31 UTC

OP

I talked to two people today about rent.

The first guy said he lives in Atlanta and pays about $2,300 a month in rent. I mentioned that works out to roughly $27,600 a year, which I personally think is a lot for rent. He also told me he was let go from a sales job at a small company about two weeks ago. Small company = low pay. When I commented on the rent being high, he responded by saying, “maybe it is a lot for you.”

I told him it is a terrible financial decision.
I also told him that I live in a major city and my monthly taxes alone are more than that.

Later, I spoke with another guy who pays around $2,000 a month for a luxury apartment. That’s about $24,000 a year. I didn’t ask about his income or job situation, but I shared that I thought the annual rent cost seemed very high.

I kept telling him his rent was a waste of money and he should buy a house. He told me he might not want to to live somewhere for so long. I asked him how he expected to build wealth. The conversation went back and forth with me saying it was a waste of “money down the drain.” He eventually admitted it was hard and bad use of money and insulted me with “life is hard when YOU don’t live in mommies basement.”
Wow.
He is literally throwing money down the drain and I trying to give me good advice.

It made me wonder if people commonly spend over $20,000–$30,000 a year on rent? And if so, how common is that relative to income and lifestyle choices?

Please look at picrel. Do people spend so much of their paycheque on rent? How does anyone become rich?

Curious what others think about this.

Early replies (first 3)

62372611
>>62372604
>I talked to two people today about rent.
no you didn't

62372621
>$24k per year rent is throwing money down the drain
>$50k per year interest payments for 30 years to "own" the same thing is building wealth

Lol

Lmao

62372624
>>62372604

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372834
28k is a lot for rent, but ppl don't want to live next to niggers or have a 3 hour commute. Everything in life is a tradeoff.

62372843
>>62372831
>A bunch of housing markets charge more for a mortgage than the local price of rent.
name three

62372877
Not reading the LLM trash, OP is niggerhomosexual

62372892
>>62372604
> maybe it is a lot for you.
> life is hard when YOU don’t live in mommies basement.
Brokies always the first to throw insults.

62372903
>>62372831
>>62372843
> A bunch of housing markets charge more for a mortgage than the local price of rent.
This makes sense, obviously. Risk management for lenders etc.
But still, name 3. I bet major desirable areas are all like this. Home ownership isn’t meant to be for anyone/everyone.

62372909
Reminder that every "renting is actually cheaper than a mortgage" result is based on mixing all rents and all mortgages together, and that if you compare the same type of unit, rent must be greater than mortgaging the same unit on average or the property owner would be bankrupt. Most property owners don't actually own their units, they forever refinance balloon loans, meaning if the sum of all the rents of their properties ever dipped bellow their initial appraisal, their loan would be called by the bank. Rent can only ever go up, and it can only ever be more expensive than the loan you're paying off for the owner.

62372913
Buying a home will always be superior, anyone who says otherwise are just poorfags who can't afford a down payment and are thus stuck renting forever.

62372918
>>62372604
I can’t even stand rentoids living near me can’t even bear to look at those subhumans fuck em

62372921
>>62372643
>The mortgage goes towards ownership. Rent is down the drain.
the $1000 cost difference goes towards 14% compounding ETFs while you still have to pay property tax and maintenance and hours of unpaid work

62372923
>>62372909
>>62372913
>>62372918
See>>62372921

62372924
>>62372604
>paycheque

62372931
>>62372921
You still pay for the property tax and maintenance, its just not itemized. No free lunch. You're not left with extra income because you're renting, but because you're renting something smaller. If you were mortgaging the same unit you'd have even more.

62372936
>>62372931
I can’t even stand to have anonymous online discourse with rentoids their low iq makes me sick.

62372937
>>62372909
People who have been renting from before 2020 are paying much less than the new renters and are winning biglly over mortgage cucks.

62372938
>>62372604
yet another nepo schizo thread about imaginary conversations

62372978
>>62372921
> hours of unpaid work
What?
There is maintenance but it isn’t even every year.

62372989
>>62372716
>he doesn't know mortgage interest is tax deductible

62373027
>>62372604
Consider padsplit

62373041
I agree that home ownership is generally the smart play compared to renting. It's a matter of building equity vs. making payments that don't equate to building more wealth. Ownership in general is also how real wealth is created.

My rent is way more than the mortgage payments of several of my friends. However, home ownership could be problematic for someone like me. The biggest issue is that you have to wait ~5 years on average to see a ROI, or so I was taught. If a job isn't working out but you just bought a house, that could be a huge problem. Then there are minor issues like needing to stay on top of repairs, realizing too late that you bought a piece of shit and that it needs a lot of work, and landscaping. Even if you are building real wealth in the long term, you might be stretched really thin for many years and you're fucked if your salary doesn't increase.

62373102
I just lost a match in Marvel Rivals.
I was born in the top 1%.
I am 40 and never had to work my whole life.

62373138
>>62372936
Their attitudes make me sick.
>not everyone lives in their moms basement
That’s what a fucking loser renter pos said to me today.
>maybe my rent it high for you
A loser brokie rentoid said this to me after I told him he is spending $27,500 a year rent.
Fucking loser pos renter class. Fuck them.
Can’t wait till shitty bug food and rent takes up 90% of their pay check.
Low iq scum.

62373151
>>62372643
>The mortgage goes towards ownership
You will have literally zero in equity until about 6 years into your mortgage.

62373163
>>62373041
>If a job isn't working out but you just bought a house, that could be a huge problem.
>
This pretty much sums up the wealth gap and why even just top 5% are are 100% out of reach for almost everyone.
You need very high income in an economy where there is no employer/employee loyalty or you need to have a high net worth already.
Top % areas are literally out of reach.
However, even basic 1m homes are out of reach for 99% of people because they require $300k income and like you said, job market is very unstable right and won’t improve.

Most people be stuck renting and never actually owning anything.
>muh investments
Okay, keep investing and growing that but the rich get even richer and you keep falling behind them.
The rich WANT people to own nothing but rent and “invest.”

62373169
>>62373151
You get the idea. But like the other anon said, it is hard hard hard because of economy and job security and it is all by design so it will never get better.
A nation of renters

62373188
>>62373163
But it's better to have some extra money while renting and enjoy some little things in life. Rich fags die just like the poor fags do anyway.

62373194
>>62373163
Yeah wealth disparity is probably just going to keep getting worse and worse. The degree to which housing prices skyrocketed after COVID is absurd and even though I could afford a cottage or a shitty townhouse, there's something disconcerting about buying something that doubled or tripled in price in just a few years. I don't think that housing prices are going to meaningfully come down though.

I'd prefer to stick to one employer but it's clear that we're just lambs for slaughter. I've gotten one raised in 4.5 years and they won't budge so it's clear that it's time to leave. I would've been fucked if I had to make mortgage payments over this time with the expectation that it would become easier but then again, my rent payments have been squeezing me dry.

Also $2000+/month is generally the going rate if you don't want to share a living quarters with section 8 tenants.

62373206
>>62373188
> Rich fags die just like the poor fags do anyway.
This is poor fag cope.
There are now life extension protocols taking away the poorfag cope that the rich die too.

62373217
>>62373194
> Also $2000+/month is generally the going rate if you don't want to share a living quarters with section 8 tenants.
The retard today was telling me his remt was $2300 a month in fucking Atlanta.
The other guy admitted his condo was luxury. Th Atlanta guy was probably just bad with money.
> there's something disconcerting about buying something that doubled or tripled in price in just a few years.
Yeah.

62373235
>>62373194
You could do what people did 100 years ago and build your own house. Ppl don't do this anymore bc we've all become soft cucks, but prefab houses are surprisingly cheap.

62373271
>>62373235
So are those homes that aren’t on wheels but they are like mobile homes. Usa has many of those, I don’t know what they are called. Everyone just wants to live in a desirable area.

item.title: WE ARE THE OILMARINES — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE ARE THE OILMARINES

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-16 01:46 UTC

OP

AND WE ARE NEVER SELLING
1k eoy

Early replies (first 3)

62361102
>>62361092
kek

62361136
My real life would have unironically began if oil pumped beyond $200. Alas.

62361150
>>62361136
I was never possible. The recession that would have started at 150 would have immediately lowered the price again. Also reserve releases, flexible demand, increase of production by usa and russia, the pipeline of the saudis and more factors were always there. I told oilschizos that but they wouldnt believe me.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361168
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62361804
Trump.. please don't take the deal.. My oil bags..

62361809
>>62361092
No. The underlying machinery would function as is now even if it were $3 a barrel.

Oil pricing is literally about how much blood you can squeeze from the peasants

62361831
>>62361092
>>62361168
>He bought dead dinosaur blood

62364177
Gulf oil output will take years to recover, and the fraud and accounting gymnastics done to keep the price low will have to be paid for. $150 EOY

62364518
>>62364177
>>62361150

62367392
>>62361092
HODL ON MARINES

62367941
>>62361092
is there a reddit community? i need a good laugh.

62367948
>>62361092
All marines are oil marines.

62367951
>>62367941
You're posting in one.

62367975
>>62361150
oil wouldn't have recessed the world at 150 , lol oil at 150 when adjusted for inflation is still under what is was by 46 dollars in 2007

62369178
>>62367975
Kek no inflation was maybe 100% since then not 220%. And yes it would have recessed the world, as it did back then. Because you can't just x3 oil and not do it. Back then the absolute numbers were higher, but the world was used to it. Now the world is used to cheap oil.

62370088
>>62364532
The singularity will happen pirate

62373205
ONE NOTHING WRONG WITH ME

62373242
>>62369178
>Kek no inflation was maybe 100% since then not 220%

wrong, jew

62373318
>>62361092
BASED

item.title: POOMP YOU'RE MISSING OUT WE'RE ALL GETTING RICH — 22 replies — 79.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
POOMP YOU'RE MISSING OUT WE'RE ALL GETTING RICH

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-16 01:31 UTC

OP

POOMP
YOU'RE MISSING OUT
WE'RE ALL GETTING RICH

Early replies (first 3)

62372990
wow their revenue is going to 200x. you would have to be a retard not to buy calls

62372997
>>62372988
lol, this is their entire plan to remove wealth from the public before the end of the world? I don't think so. SPCX isn't even worth the current price. Go look at what they did on the futures markets. SPCX has already been listed for markets as a futures contract asset and its chart completely dumped from 2,200 to 160.

62373002
The man who owns the company saying its going to go up!? OMG BUY LADS

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62373004
>>62373002
This. its a complete scam being used to mass wealth so they can buy a shit load of bitcoin

62373008
>>62372997
>corporate share split and index rebasing that occurred ahead of the historic SpaceX (SPCX) IPO.
Shill

62373065
>>62372988
So how many shares to buy? How many needed to make it

62373067
>CEO: i think my company is worth infinity dollarydoos
>investors: HOLY FUCK BUY NOW

goyim are retards who should be enslaved

62373068
>>62372988
if a singular company doing mostly meaningless economic activity is able to actually create $1T revenue in a singular business year around 2030;
simply imagine how much trillions of revenue essential economical endeavors must be making within the same time period. (probably quire a lot)

now hypothetically try to extrapolate from this the amount of outstanding currency/debt/credit roaming around the economy directly and how high asset prices would be, which are nearly 100% of the time are prone to derive their value from the total outstanding currency/debt/credit.

one would come to the conclusion that this would mean there is mind boggling, reality bending inflation in both currency/debt/credit AND THEREFORE consumer prices and asset prices ahead.

now remember how well you were faring with actual inflation in the past.

yeah.

good luck.

62373084
>>62373067
none of the investors believe this, they all just think that they can time the market and sell thier garbage to a more credulous retard

62373086
>>62373068
>reddit spacing
i bet you said the same about tesla

62373112
>>62373086
you seem confused.

perhaps you are confusing stock price/market cap and revenue.

62373124
>>62372988
this sort of bullshit on social media should be illegal to say by the owner of the company. blatant price manipulation.

62373142
You're laughing now. You won't be laughing in 2030 when Tyrone and Rajesh fly to Mars on US debt.

62373147
>>62373124
It is illegal, but the current admin won’t prosecute it.

62373152
>>62372988
Im less poor than I was yesterday
Thanks Elon

62373154
>>62373112
i trust Elon over some random doom poster

62373176
>>62373154
Because you are castrated goyim

62373183
>>62373176
Youre just seething because you didn't buy the IPO and are Sidelined for the golden bull run (again)

62373215
>>62373008
None of this happened. I'm talking about SPCX futures being listed on Coinbase months before this cattle IPO launch and hype. So now that SPCX IPO'ed, what does Coinbase do? They hide the previous futures chart to reflect the current IPO stock on the futures chart when the fucking futures chart showed they started at 2,200 in liquid contracts to than just dump to 160. Thats why the IPO was at 160. Its a giant fucking scam. Elon's personal piggy bank.

62373236
so where is the money gonna flow when the rugging starts? i already took out my initial gamble play now im just riding house money, all in DRAM again?

62373255
>>62373004
The word you're looking for is "amass" you esl dipshit. remove yourself from my board NOW

62373272
>>62373255
>The word you're looking for is "amass" you esl dipshit. remove yourself from my board NOW
Is this a spelling bee, you pedantic little shit? kys

item.title: It has begun — 37 replies — 57.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
It has begun

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-16 01:09 UTC

OP

SpaceX pre-market starts with UP movement

Early replies (first 3)

62369167
good

i own one share of spcx

62369204
+5% LOCKED IN

169-170$ in pre-market
up from 160$ at friday close

62369215
>>62369156
Am i the only one concerned with all this speculation over an asset that has little intrinsic value?
Everybody has their eyes in this shit. Something else must be the thing to buy right now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369295
>>62369264
ok, faggot boomer

62369373
>>62369252
Every mainstream media outlet is shilling this thing hard and yet anons are walking right into the trap like deers in headlights.

62369439
Ask yourself how many times you'd be willing to play russian roulette with 1 bullet and 100 chambers.

62369442
>>62369439
99 times

62369447
>>62369442

62369548
>>62369373
They're shilling it because indices have to buy a total combined 250 million shares (40% float) over the upcoming weeks. The more they can pump it, the more money they make.

62369568
>>62369156
I'm never touching it

62369572
>>62369156
>just kidding goy, back to work now!

62369613
>>62369572
good I'm still accumulating

62369620
>>62369223
TSLA soon

62369632
>>62369613
same I hope it drops back into the 150s at least, so I can buy with leverage when SPCH is open to buy

62369635
>>62369613
STOP BUYING YOU MODERBITCH!! WHY DONT YOU UNDERTAND SIR? SPCX IS OVERVALUED SIR. SIR YOU NEED TO SHORT, NOT BUY SIR!!

62369672
>>62369613
Sure you are. Post notarized and audited broker statements pronto.

62369679
>>62369672

62369688
>>62369679
That's proof you goofed into the IPO alloc, not proof that you've bought a single share since then. Notarized and audited, btw. You're just milking your bags.

62369703
>>62369688
>instant profit
>goofed
stay poor

62369735
>>62369703
>still accumulating
We still haven't seen where you scooped the remainder of those 1000 shares you asked for.
>notarized
and
>audited

62369981
>>62369156
>>62369735
>>62369703
LIFTOFF!
173 $

62370031
baggies about to get BTFO, like all IPO's.

62370155
The rocket is not going up

62370179
>>62369167
>mfw I only bought 7
I should of bought more bros

62370206
>>62370179
It's not too late.

62370723
ONE HUNDRED SEVENTY.SEVEN

62370740
>>62369442
Kekd

62370753
I don't buy hype, even if it works. Betting on the stupidity of investors is always a road to disaster.

62370827
Just remember /biz/ did hardcore shill movements against getting SPCX. You know what /biz/ is now. The TAM says we are going to 22k a share.

62370863
>>62369735
Go back to retardit to seethe over Elon faggot

62370892
>>62370827
You are literally the embodiment of /biz though.

62373182
>>62370827
The TAM that divides up as over $20,000 per year from every single human being on the planet being spent on SpaceX? More than people spend on their food and housing? Yeah, any day now we'll all start shelling out our monthly $1700 SpaceX subscriptions for... Uhh...

62373198
>>62373182
i saw a 20 million dollar buy order go thru
and 20k is the exact amound my dad spent, we were on the phone and both skeptical but somehow both ended up talking ourselves into buying some

i only have 5

item.title: So when is SPCX going to crash? — 28 replies — 15.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
So when is SPCX going to crash?

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-16 00:01 UTC

OP

> Next FOMC meeting
> Next earnings date
> Right when it gets into the Nasdaq100
> ?
There is no way that a company priced in hopium that actually loses money is not going to crash like a shitcoin at some point.

Early replies (first 3)

62366003
>>62365854
When is TSLA going to crash?

Musk can string out the SPCX hopium even longer than the TSLA hopium, possibly for decades.

62366023
>>62365854
I expect that every test launch of a SuperHeavy/Starship will create an options trader's dream.
* rocket blows up, stock price plunges
* test goes perfectly, stock price zooms

So, just trade straddles starting a day or two before every big event.

Ever seen Boeing's stock price after a crash? One plane goes down, whole stock drops. Never mind that it's usually either defective maintenance or the pilots' fault.

62366153
>>62365854
Look at this as you'd look at a shitcoin TGE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366334
>>62366153
Shitcoins crash so fucking hard when they do.

62366341
>>62366003
Tesla actually makes money, Spacex is a bunch of AI space datacenters dream bullshit. Only Starlink makes any money in it.

62366378
It will never be below 135. Only low iq brainlests that don’t understand how important mass/cost to orbit is and how important orbital refueling is think it like mega epic overvalued

Oh and SpaceX currently has no heavy lift competition. Only Amazon is in the field and their rocket exploded recently, they are years behind. Other competitors like rklb and fly can barely do small lift. Rklb is still working on neutron to compete with falcon on medium lift. SpaceX is a monopoly currently that will unlock the next frontier. But yeah you’re right, there is nothing to do in space and its just going to crash

62366436
>>62366378
Nigga once the money tap breaks down the market gets fucking fair very fucking quick.
Tesla has done -75% in the past.

62366443
>>62366003
Tesla makes cars, ai, robots, charging stations, solar panels, power batteries and electrical grid technology.
It wont.

62366451
>>62366436
Okay? It has also do 25000% growth since 2013. Your rebuttal?

62366518
>>62366451
The point is to figure out when it is going to crash to buy very low. If you want to get it now that is trading at infinite pe ratio go ahead.

62366779
>>62365854
Even if its not vaporware, these projects all take decades. There will be multiple overall market crashes/correctioba before they get anything done. Being a single stock, its gonna crash more often then the whole market. 100% chance of a dump right before any big got contracts are announces too so insiders can load up.

62366790
When all of Reddit and biz thinks something’s gonna crash, it’s probably gonna moon

Not in the stock but am now tempted for a short term position

62367082
>>62365854
The index inclusion narrative is getting frontrun. SPCX will go 30% up from its IPO price and go down from there

62367245
>>62365854
Just wait for another rocket explosion.
Buy the dip.
This shit is so easy.

62369275
>>62366378
Sending shit to space doesn't rely on the value of stocks. It's going to trade up and down. It will probably dump soon when people are allowed to sell and/or when the earnings come in.

Buy when it comes down. If they can actually successfully make data centers in space the value will moon.

62369319
>>62366790
This. I bought hundreds of shares based solely on Reddit tears.

62369336
>>62365854
i give it a couple of weeks

62369347
Retail are idiots that buy no matter what because they have no hope of a future otherwise. Fundamentals literally don't matter anymore, really they haven't since 2023

62369355
the only way it crashes is if the 10 million starlink users stop using it and that doesn't sound plausible, i mean when califortards started burning teslas, the stock went down but came back up because it was a nothing burger local skirmish, it turns out politics and business don't mix, don't let the leftard musk hate shilling inform your financial decisions, most of them are delusional hypocrites, very few of them--in fact this proves there's very few of them--have any practical convictions

62369365
>>62365854
Space X is hopeless endeavor since moment when Elon decided land on tower!

13 or so flights and not even Orbit?
Once lucky landing?
Since nothing ..except blowing up money!

Meantime Blue Origin despite latest seatback Land on second try and deploy satellite?

X is just coupium !

Elon not going even to Moon anywhere beyond 2050!

It is all hype and money scam !
You should understand that by now!

62370328
>>62369365
Retarded plebbit spacing opinion.

>SpaceX has achieved over 670 successful launches across its rocket families, maintaining one of the highest launch cadences and success rates in aerospace history.

>Falcon 9: The world's most active workhorse has launched 662 times with a 99.55% success rate. Its active version, the Falcon 9 Block 5, boasts an even higher 99.83% success rate, with only one single in-flight failure across hundreds of flights

>Falcon Heavy: Composed of three Falcon 9-derived cores, this heavy-lift vehicle has completed all of its operational flights with a 100% success rate since its highly publicized 2018 debut.

>Starship: The massive, fully reusable super-heavy launcher is actively undergoing rapid, iterative test flights. The May 2026 inaugural test flight of the upgraded Starship Version 3 (V3) successfully launched, deployed 20 dummy satellites, and completed a controlled atmospheric re-entry and splashdown in the Indian Ocean

62370340
>>62370328
? Profit

62370936
>>62365854
Why to people buy businesses that make no money? Where do they think does the money come from?

62371151
>>62366378
You sound retarded. The way spaceX is priced it’s an AI startup with a rocket hobby. The valuation is fucking crazy and rigged to manipulate passive index funds. It’s absolutely priced in at best case scenario future happening. OpenAI is going to do the same thing. I’m absolutely furious this shit was given the green light

62371179
>>62370328
Daily reminder only 4.3% of space X shares are publicly traded. Enjoy your future rugpull

62372848
>>62371179
Well yeah, that was in the docs. The question is how many of the next available 7 percent are put up for sale.

62372930
>>62365854
Warsh will hike rates and send this to zero along with every other plebbit-tier meme stock

62372956
>>62365854
>So when is SPCX going to crash?

When SpaceX clears $1400 a share, you will SCREAMING:
"It is going to CRASH... SOON!"

item.title: You guys arent buying the fucking thing literally named DOT during the biggest bull market since the DOT COM bubble??? — 7 replies — 7.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
You guys arent buying the fucking thing literally named DOT during the biggest bull market since the DOT COM bubble???

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-16 00:50 UTC

OP

W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-

I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-

AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY

ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368461
>>62365659

62369006
>>62359336
Ok but why would you do that? Because you are jealous? Or did the rich person rape your mother or something?

62369012
>>62359336
Violence is an incredibly brown phenomen. Just vote socialists into power who will pass tax increases on the rich.

62369030
>>62365772
If we’re being honest here, everyone knows that national socialism type thing is the best system. It doesn’t even need to be tied to just one ethnicity (and nation states actually almost always have different ethnicities living in them, in Germany for example southern germans are mixed with celts and eastern germans with slavs). We could easily have EU level social democracy with tightly regulated, ”only pick the best” kind of immigration.

62369034
>>62369030
*best system for common people i mean. For the elites, USA kind of system with international tax havens is the best.

62369216
>>62359336
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62369231
Let's just recognize this as the pure envy posting that it is. Do nothing drug addicted troon is mad that somebody who is responsible for funding the lives of who knows how many hundreds of thousands of people/families by creating industry and jobs is mad that this person has earned more capital with which they will doubtless continue providing jobs. OP probably personally benefits in ways they aren't even aware of.

62369550
>>62359336
>>62359417
>>62359496
>>62364075
cute

62370324
>>62368461
Reddit is still cringe but unironically got pretty redpilled and revolutionary yeah
They seem more likely do something than 4chan chuds

62370331
>>62359336
cope

62370338
>>62370331
The real cope is asslicking the powerful and kicking down. Wanting to overthrow your slavers is the next step towards liberty at least

62370434
>>62370324
>>62370338
>reddit
>redpilled
>not wanting to destroy literally everything is asslicking
If you are so absolutely hell bent on making this fantasy a reality, why don't you just go start a commune and make it happen? Why does it need to involve murdering people, gaining literally no part of their immense 'stolen' wealth, and giving nothing back to the supposed oppressed workers?
If the utopia can only be built with violence, it will only be upheld with ultraviolence. You won't be the most violent or vicious party around, BTW.

62370741
>>62370434
how do you think our current society is upheld?

62370770
Lmao actual cryptokikes and kikes defending the rich

62370820
>>62361047
>rich
>intelligence
Only room in your bag for one, I'm afraid.

62370830
>>62362126
>let the smart people do the things that make society go
This is what speculators actually believe.

62371364
>>62370741
>upheld
It....isn't?
We had a wildly successful capitalistic system in the 'west' for the last 300 years, and a facsimile of one for the last century (thanks smoke and mirrors fiat), with increasingly onerous compliance demands by the state that start to decouple free markets toward state operated markets (which is far closer to the current paradigm). State regulated markets fucking suck and are a constantly changing landscape of chosen winners and losers based on which group is in power at any one time. Markets are wavering and unstable, currencies are starting to decouple, and global stability is starting to crack under the weight of unnatural (government mandated) migration and capital flows.
Don't ask disingenuous questions, for they open a two way window into your mind (which isn't a compliment)

62371408
>>62359336
thats why we gate keep niggers like that away from us via invitation only or simply not walking near them or anywhere around them your response? ever see batman?

62371411
>>62359336
im basically batman

62371435
>>62365552
I made it. From living in a 1975 Ford maverick to having land and houses and a wife and 3 kids. Go fucking jump, you're a useless eater and only a burden to the rest of us. Fucking pathetic faggot.

>>62371408

Don't let us see you in any cities or public areas

62371441

62371449

62371459

WONT
DO

62365955

62366586

62369541

62370167

62370371
>>62365514

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 100 replies — 14.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-06-15 18:56 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

62364599
>>62339349
>Time to start acting like an african american.
finally someone said it

62365449
>>62362661
You're just doing the right thing..

62365460
any time you can cash out, turn a profit and not get anally blasted by exchanges is a good time to cash out.

62368338
>>62364599
Damn

62369180
Obviously my >$100k sells were the "right move". Cycle theory helped a lot with that one. If I had the clarity of hindsight, I would have sold more. Bitcoin is amazing tech but its potential is as-yet unrealized. Holding it doesn't do much for anyone by itself. Sure, people say you can borrow against it or maybe get yield somewhere - fuck that shit. I need to see a thriving system of exchange based on Bitcoin replacing the current economy before I keep it around for practical reasons.
Until that point, I trade dollars for Bitcoin, Bitcoin for more dollars, and dollars for more Bitcoin. How I trade it is based on the likelihood of reaching that final evolutionary stage, and right now it's looking pretty dire. What the establishment wants is to turn Bitcoin into a exotic monetary instrument held for bragging points and dick measurement reasons, and that's what people like Saylor and ETFs are doing, and why OGs are dumping on their faces. What I want to see more of is trustless stateless peer-to-peer transactions involving real shit like commodities, cars, houses etc. which seems to be happening through Monero.

Anyways I'm hoping to buy back lower around August-September. Maybe $35-40k. Thanks for reading my diary.

62371627
>>62365460
time will tell.

item.title: Did Elon Musk Know the Iran Deal Was Coming? — 14 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Did Elon Musk Know the Iran Deal Was Coming?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-16 00:49 UTC

OP

>The timing is hard to ignore. SpaceX's historic IPO launched trading on June 12th, two days before the Trump administration announced a peace agreement with Iran that sent markets soaring, and that sequence may not be coincidental. The theory goes like this: the Iran war, for all its apparent chaos in the press, was operating on a quiet internal deadline that the administration had set as far back as February, with mid-June as the outer limit for wrapping up the conflict. The public back-and-forth between Iran's foreign minister crying foul over "maximalist demands" and Lindsey Graham expressing concern on the day of the announcement was negotiating theater, not a reflection of what was actually happening behind closed doors. The real agreement was likely reached in early June, with two weeks of runway used for final language, internal political management, and choosing the right public moment. That's also when someone in the administration, in all likelihood, told Musk the plan. SpaceX is not a normal company. It's a $1.75 trillion national security asset, deeply embedded with DoD and US intelligence, and there's a legitimate government interest in its IPO going well. Musk, armed with a specific date, then steered the IPO's still-flexible timeline to land on June 12th: one trading day before the weekend the deal went public, perfectly positioned to catch the rally. None of this is provable. But the alternative asks a lot of coincidence: that the largest IPO in stock market history just happened to land in the two-day window before a major geopolitical resolution.

Early replies (first 3)

62372274
henlo i wrote this think piece
what do u think?

62372277
>>62372263
are we ignoring the 53 times trump has announced a deal before and it didn't happen?

62372281
>>62372263
Pretty sure the IPO was in the investment bank pipeline before the Iran conflict even began.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372307
>>62372277
Yes

62372321
I think its more likely trump was aware of the largest ipo in history and signed to not fuck it

62372333
>>62372263
I don’t even need to read all of that to know the answer is yes

62372336
>>62372274
This image brings me hope

62372351
>>62372263
There was plenty of signal that it was only a matter of time. For weeks, there were reports of unpaid work and possible water rationing in iran. I get the counterpoint that the regime and their supporters view this as a holy war but the most loyal supporters will eventually capitulate once they start to starve.

62372381
>>62372274
Reads like (and probably is) AI slop

62372743
>>62372274
Very interesting and spot on analysis fren

62372890
>>62372263
Everybody except the
>Trump always TACOs out
crowd knew the deal was coming. Iran couldn't ship its oil out past the strait, but the US-aligned gulf states could ship their oil out past the strait (albeit at a slightly reduced rate) so it was always a matter of time before Iran caved.

62372917
>>62372743
samefagging jeet ai

62373108
>>62372917
Wrong anon

62373114
>>62372263
>Did drumpf insider trade for the eighty second time?

I guess

OP

>2 TRILLION MARPET KAP
>U R HER
>3 TRILLIONAIRES
>10 TRILIONIES
>100 TRILLIONZES

Early replies (first 3)

62372684
Strap in, we're going to the moon.

62372691
>>62372673
Can anyone tell me exactly what space x is supposed to be doing?
Mining on the moon?

62372703
>>62372691
I can't believe this needs to be explained but the answer is AI. They said they will build data centers in space and that's all that is needed.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62372710
>>62372691
Mining on mars with robots. No regulatory agencies have the technology to regulate on mars so the sheer number of resources that can be obtained will be substantial. The moon is to be a launchpad.

62372747
>>62372703
>>62372710
>data centers in space
>robot mining on Mars
Amazing, does that happen before or after we get fully autonomous self-driving teslas? Maybe he'll build a hyperloop, but for rockets - that way the minerals are transported faster. I'm so heckin excited guys

62372748
>>62372673
He was right, SpaceX really is driving the goyim insane. They can't handle it and are making threads like this now.

62372768
>>62372691

62372772
>>62372691
lining the pockets of institutional investors

62372779
>>62372747
It looks like famine is next. Anyhoo, the datacenter craze is just a thing to allocate land and utilities to future projects. Most of those won't actually be data centers. A lot of them are going to be neocities. Others to be massive industrial plants. It's just this way they can be allocated funds and space without having to go into details about their data center that isn't a data center project. One of the jokes is that the emissions and usage data used to smear data centers are actually just city data. So it's people arguing against having cities because of the environmental impact.

62372794
>>62372691
SpaceX(and all other X companies which Elon wants to eventually merge together) is trying to become become the irl Wayland Yutani, excluding all the biotech/synth/ayylmao stuff

62372825
>>62372794
>excluding all the biotech/synth/ayylmao
It'll definitely include biotech and synths (neuralink and XAI)

62372894
200, 300 next losers lmao

62372951
>>62372691
>Can anyone tell me exactly what space x is supposed to be doing?
printing infinite money, apparently

>Amazing, does that happen before or after we get fully autonomous self-driving teslas? Maybe he'll build a hyperloop, but for rockets - that way the minerals are transported faster. I'm so heckin excited guys
lamao
maybe he can first make a non-crap car (here on Earth)

62373030
>>62372673
Honestly it could go to $1k. Why? Because only 5% of the shares are being traded. It's a trillion dollar market cap but only trading $100 billion worth of shares.
It will only be unsustainable for companies to pump it when they start unlocking the smelly 20% and 28% investor lockups.
The first days when only 5% of shares are traded will be the most profitable for companies to scam pump and pin the price higher because it's easier to do it.

62373088
>>62372673
this but unironically

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 108 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 108 • Updated: 2026-06-15 15:56 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364918
>>62361478
He threw a pretty good fastball that one time. It made more progress than the vehicle did thoughbeit.

62365050
>>62361452
because stocks don't behave rationally and memes move the market more strongly than financial schizobabble

62365078
>>62361452
you can become one too, just create a crypto coin, mint 1T tokens and sell 1 for $1 on the open markets

62365099
>>62361452
heres the thing. it was all vaporware the whole time...tesla included. it just wasnt as brazen as the ipo before...worse with every scam. so he could keep borrowing. because hes doing "buy borrow die", it became the banks problem at this point because hes never gonna pay them back, he couldnt if he wanted to, he couldnt if they tried to liquidate. they are heavily incentivized to help him dump the baggies on you to close out these transactions. then he can have a fake ketamine overdose and go party with jeffjeff again.

62365104
>>62361452
elon is one of the only men actually creating value in this world.

almost the entire economy is pure pointless bullshit
>delivering sneakers to bestial niggers
>selling sloppa to bestial niggers
>keeping morbidly obese 100 year old bestial niggers alive
>manufacturing weapons so bestial niggers can shoot each other and blow shit up
>making handbags for the bestial femboons

elon is pursuing the future, the transcendence. he deserves to win.

62365105
>>62365104
They are going to deepfake it all just like nasa with kubrik except this time you paid for it by buying the ipo instead of pillaged wwii wealth

62365111
>>62365105
you are simply a low iq schizoid. you're exactly like the npcs except you're against the current thing.

62365115
>>62365111
You got mkultrad because your uncle molested you while you watched star trek or some other faggot shit and you liked it. Now spaecekeks launches make you feel cummies

62365124
>>62365115
very complex projection. must be something personal.

62365130
>>62365124
Keep fantacizing about scifi bullshit that will never happen. I hope you buy a neuralink and end up like terry schiavo

62365216
>>62365130
low iq npcs such as yourself cannot conceive of something which is not the current thing. you have my pity.

62365235
>>62361452
vibe investing.

62365240
>>62361452
it's because Musk is a cult leader

62365247
>>62361459
Literally what is "dystopian" about swarms of AI powered assassin drones?

62366996
>>62361471
I wish I could make a trillion doing nothing

62367047
Space X is worth 2 trillion...because it just is ok!

62367049
>>62363603
Tesla cars tend to burst in flames more than the most insignificant chink car company.

62367163
>>62361452
Elon Cuck

62367477
>>62361549
lol, >>62361539 can't handle the truth

62367557
every major technological transformation in last 15 years is due to elon.

electric cars = tesla = elon
biohacking = neuralink = elon
space and global broadban = spacex = elon
fixing transporation = boring = elon
AI = openAI (elon founded it) + grok = elon
social media = X = elon

that's why he's a trillionaire and ur a nobody posting a forum when you should be grinding like elon who regularily sleeps on factory floors.

62367687
>>62367557
>gayest cars
>dead monkeys
>global 5G
>jew tunnels
>sloppa
>Jeets
Musk

62367701
>>62367687
Missed the IPO thunderf00t?

62370282
>>62362688
>he received massive subsidies the entire time in tesla
so did everyone else, you braindead commie
t. not a fan

>>62362688
>he received massive
you fucking retarded monkey. commiefornia gave out money left and right for the electric and environmental revolution and he simply lined up. tf is wrong with you. probably a retarded zoomer or something

just because he didn't burn his cash like the entire gaming industry, doesn't make his success not something he didn't earn

you're angry because he would spit in your face and laugh at you if you came up to him with something retarded.
he probably wouldn't, he would save his energy and simply walk away and ignore you like the insignificant vermin that you are

HEYO RETARD ALERT

62370333
>>62362688
here, retarded monkey, because i'm in a good mood
>i forgot to include US-only because i always forget LLMs are only slightly less retarded than you

lol
>i can pull up
yes please do

tesla elon musk subsidies

62370351
>>62362557
>>62363104
>Poorfag doesn't bussines
kek 100%

it's so easy to spot them. the funny thing is they don't even know it is
3 words is all it takes. that's a skill!

62370386
There's quite literally only so many things to invest in and Elon is one of the few guys that make absolutely insane bets on tech that sounds like science fiction. It doesn't matter if he said he'd be sending people to Mars 10 years ago, the fact that his company is constantly pushing forward is impressive. Coming up with insane ideas like putting data centers is space might be completely retarded but he still spends the money for someone to sit down and figure out a less insane version of that and every so often that bet pays off. This is exactly the kind of thing that the US did during WW2 and the cold war and its how they ended up landing on the moon, build the Internet, the blackbird, develop nuclear bombs as well as nuclear energy, and so on. The only problem with Elon is that he's a cringe spergy attention whore.

62370487
>>62370386
Oh and his obsession with H1B indians is bad as well.

62370524
>>62361478
He doesn't even play poe2 lmao. He literally streamed poe2 to thousands of people on a character he paid a chinese guy to level up, so that he could appear on top of the hardcore ladder.
What kind of a man does that? Who the fuck would think that is sane? He literally does blatant shit like this, lies to your face.

62370572
>>62361478
xai literally hasn't done anything

62370604
>>62370487
he already got bodied for his opinion in late 2024. everyone did
https://x.com/yacineMTB/status/1871948474075484409
>https://x.com/UnfiltdTruth/status/1871959163506434077
https://x.com/dmitry140/status/1871985484370678007
>https://x.com/ParikPatelCFA/status/1871989514853597529
https://x.com/kangminjlee/status/1872013129112268848
>https://x.com/BowTiedRanger/status/1872026072826400816
https://x.com/asharoraa/status/1872041603872997614
>https://x.com/Suhail/status/1872053488928309588
https://x.com/4Evashook/status/1872103003249168503
>https://x.com/TheMagaHulk/status/1872105922677387684
https://x.com/Nithya2102/status/1872110384427176236
>https://x.com/DanielDiMartino/status/1872116639510241386

i had so many more but i don't know where i have the rest -.-

i haven't looked into it much since then but it's a political national thing anyway. if it's there why not use it. i see enough whities during all these launches
same reason why you would want to deploy tariffs. matter of priorities. do you value soul or sludge

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 55 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE…

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-06-15 19:58 UTC

OP

how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEET would they be fine with it.. probably not i think they want you to be grinding your job 24/7 and making like 400k a year

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366005
>>62365587
>>62365738
Do the math OP.

62366027
>>62365989
>have all this
>become her bf
>she cheats on you with a nigga that has NOTHING of this

62366041
>>62365608
Animal should be allowed to just breed and leave, there is nothing like third parties in the interaction.

62366052
>>62365557
>dude just get herpes with a romanian gypsy in a shitty hotel in the suburbs

kys

62366054
>Women or leveraged, margined silver miners?
Mmmmm...... you're gonna have to get back to me on that bro

62366257
>>62365587
>>62365738
>>62366005
^ This system is outdated, but I've invented a new one. The equation is identical, but the compounding effects of the numerator have been upscaled, personality remains the same. Proximity now has a negative and positive value.

62366581
>>62366052
Yep, sounds incredibly based

62366590
>>62365989
Delusion
Just look at families walking around

62366599
>>62365958
>>62365953
Do you know any married men
And those things can be deal breakers, there are lines/limits

62366630
>>62365540
the only girls that care about careers in 2026 are egg freezing hags. you need just enough money to get to where women are (like the bar or anywhere else) and live somewhere. anything past that is diminishing returns.

62366635
anon don't listen to desperate insecure virgins for dating advice
the world is full of poor women who will date you if you have a good personality
when my wife and I started dating, I was literally unemployed and living with my parents
Now I make more than her but it took me 5 years to get there.

62366639
>>62365555
>I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection

Dad?

62366677
>>62365608
all of these women made a different man spend thousands and wait months before fucking really makes you ponder

62366728
>>62366599
well eventually these kinds of women also tend to be single mothers

62367007
>>62365897
Yes paradoxically the girls that are most wife material aren't the ones going around looking to date. I think if you are out of school you're out of luck
>Girlfriend through connections
I know this sounds stupid, but I'd feel like a cuck if I met my girlfriend through a mutual.
Think about it, MY girlfriend would have been interested in talking to someone else FIRST. I wouldn't want to date a girl with guy friends anyways

Impossible propositions are abound

62367017
>>62366635
Do the math.

62367050
>>62367007
Yeah, that’s pretty retarded.
There are plenty of non-sexual reasons why a guy might know some other girl, like a family member, girlfriend/wife’s friend, former co-worker, former school friend.
You could make friends with girls to try to date one of their friends too.
The crux is on your ability to be a normal person to any normal person regardless of gender or age.
That’s how you find girls who aren’t sluts and divorce rapists. Not how you sleep around though.
So you have to figure out if what you really want is someone to have sex with regularly, or someone to start a family with. Because it’s a lot of social effort.

62367055
>>62367007
I just realized, you may have severely low self esteem if you can’t handle the thought of a gf talking to someone else before you.
Low self esteem is girl repellant
Fix that first

62367080
>>62365555
Checked and damn feels bad, I hope you make it.

>>62365540
Former sex haver here, if she loves you not much. Unironically you could go have a picnic with some bullshit like pizza, or go on a road trip. Strategic use of money is ideal. Don't just buy flowers, get them delivered to her work so all her coworkers get pissed and jealous. You want enough money to lock down a nice wedding and kids but thats really it, this was my fatal mistake. I have enough for three weddings now but opportunities are gone for me.

62367870
I get 1k CAD neetbux and do just fine
If not a demo thread, you're overthinking it retard

62367927
>>62365555
unironically, where are the lazy NEET gfs?

62368381
>>62365555
I just want to watch gun boat ball 2 wit my aspie unicorn believing gf turned housewife mommy with fear of scrutiny. I guess i was born too late

62368990
>>62367050
>>62367055
Think about it though. If a girl has tons of guy friends, that's a pretty clear no signal right?
>Low self esteem
true :(

62369133
>>62368990
>>62368990
You can make friends with women too anon. Women that you aren't interested in can have friends that you are interested in.

Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.

Unironically truely befriend a lesbian, this will teach you how to act around women without acting like a retard as you won't try to fuck her.

Also if you're so insecure talk to a therapist/take shrooms and start working out. Its all ego that you have to let go in the end.

Good luck fren!

62369150
All the matches I got on tinder is 30s post wall roast, I can’t believe the 28yi tinder matches something literally call me old hag when I’m just a 35yo bald, obese, needy, short, no STEM degree, and potentially impotent.

62369226
>>62365540
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62369807
>>62365540
Elon is worth $1,000,000,000,000 and still can't find a woman that wants to stay with him

62371591
>>62369133
> take shrooms
Based

>>62368990
You’re letting your insecurities delude you.

62371912
>>62365555
you sound like a retard

62371972
>>62369133
>Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.
even if this is the case the guy most likely wants to fuck them you retard.

part of being in a relationship is having a basic level of respect for your partner. that means dropping all single behaviour, so no hanging out 1 on 1 with the opposite sex, no texting other guys, and no getting drunk without you in bars/clubs. its not insecurity to expect a the most basic of relationship boundaries.

>just take drugs bro!
>go pay money to a millenial woman
holy bluepilled retard

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 32 replies — 9.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-15 22:14 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350015
>>62349960
Eh. I think they'll just turn shit up to 11 because Bank of America lost.

62352238
biz, so far my prediction is working, stop posting on 1pbtid pepe threads, and let me know if my predictions are shit or not

62353716
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's

62353764
>>62353716
It'll be a lost decade. It'll take 5-7 years for first new nuclear reactors to come online to unclog the the backlog of ai data centers that will be getting shit canned later this year or next after the crash.

Commodities will be the main play. World needs real resources to build infrastructure and robots. Shit doesn't mine itself out of the ground.

62353866
>>62353764
thanks, yea, seems to be that way. Right around 1973 market peak to 1975ish, gold picked up a 2x bid, so I imagine other commodities would follow suit

It's hard to imagine though, because I look around and I still to this day don't see labor inflation, yet everyone is speaking about how all this build out is happening. If it's happening, why doesn't it show up in labor? That's the first bottleneck that'll show up, no? It showed up in 08, are we saying all the mining and manufacturing just simply doesn't require that many humans? That seems a little bit farfetched to me

62355245
>>62349960
elf

62359675
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's x2

62359708
Sex with Frieren

62359749
>>62359675
what do you want to hear? the market will go up or go down. your doom pronouncement is stupid since people have been predicting the apocalypse for thousands of years and have always been wrong. also LLMs are gay and AI companies deserve to be buried in piracy and antitrust lawsuits by the next administration. I hope every AI ceo ends up in jail

62359780
>>62359708
Sex with this anon

62359931
>>62359749
fuck yourself, im gonna keep snailposting till you retards give me some concrete nostradamus dates and counter reasoning exactly like you retards do on the 1pbtid pepe threads

62359962
Line will continue up until 7/4/28
7*4 = 28 and they get the satisfaction of destroying a holiday. Will also hand everything to the dems in the next election.

62359991
>>62349960
This is now a Flielen thread

62359998
>>62359991

62361834
snailposting for continued feedback, you're not getting rid of me that easily

62361911
>>62349960
Woah wait a second Frieren, the FOMC is next week? Fucccccccckkkk I need to deleverage.
>FOMC -> NDQ goes up
Walk me through this part if they hike wouldn't that cause a dump or something not more pumping? So should I hold my bullish positions or not? Also I'm pretty sure both Anthropic and OpenAI ipo this year in that order.

62361941
>>62353866
this year I saw a lot of ads for oil related jobs masquerading aa youtube shorts
not sure if that counts

62363423
>>62361911
when trump said he'll be signing the agreement on sunday, the target rates dumped

https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/interest-rates/cme-fedwatch-tool.html

warsh is gonna come out next week and be dovish, but the market will act like its not simply because they're range trading, and they're gonna rebuy their positions.

62364277
motheryuckers, this snail post will exist for years

62364284
>>62349960
>metabolized
Stop reading right there.

62366455
>>62364284

don't worry, illiterate retard opinions aren't needed

62366592
>>62366455
I didn't give an opinion.

62368882
snailposting till nostradamus pomp or domp predictions

62370523
snailposting is just transient guys

62370546
>>62370523
Reddit elf

62370557
>>62370546
tard

62370592
>>62370523

How certain are you about that November election? Rs literally gerrymandered the south and killed D seats + added 12. Trump picks have won every primary solidifying his control. I think it will be a narrow rig in order for the country to not go "troubles" Dem or Rep doesn't fucking matter, probably Rep +1 in the house

62372434
>>62370592
hmm good point, but there's no way they can unfuck the public opinion by november. The pain of inflation will be too fresh, and the memory of Iran causing that pain to boot. All the poor people will be out in force

62372555
>>62359780
Is Frieren a virgin?

Thanks for this thread OP
